name: Croa Trail
description: Enjoy an easy 3.9-mile circular hike at Las Salinas de Ulla, exploring salt flats, historic ruins, and views of Lake Castiñeiras.
region_intro: Hiking around Vilaboa offers diverse natural landscapes, including riverside trails, dense forests, and coastal areas. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, from the lush banks of the Tomeza River to the unique environment of the Ulló Salt Flats. Historical landmarks are often integrated into the hiking paths, providing cultural points of interest. Best hiking trails around Vilaboa The most popular hiking route is Croa Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ulló Salt Flats loop from Arcade, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This circular trail offers views of salt flats and historical ruins. Local hikers also love the Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Río Tomeza Riverside Trail loop from Arcade, an 17.9 miles (28.9 km) trail leading through riverine and forested areas, often completed in about 7 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Vilaboa is defined by riverine paths, coastal scenery, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Vilaboa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 5,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Vilaboa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vilaboa?
A: Vilaboa offers a diverse network of 13 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the region's unique landscapes, from riverine paths to coastal areas.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Vilaboa?
A: Hikers in Vilaboa can experience a rich variety of landscapes. You'll find lush riverside trails along the Tomeza River, dense forests, and coastal scenery, including the unique environment of the Ulló Salt Flats. The region's terrain is quite varied, offering something for every nature enthusiast.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Vilaboa?
A: Yes, Vilaboa has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. There are 8 routes classified as easy, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375730094/croa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croa Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.3 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14934538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats loop from Arcade&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path offering views of salt flats and historical ruins.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Vilaboa?
A: Yes, Vilaboa features several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14934538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats loop from Arcade&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile (5.3 km) trail. For a more challenging circular experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Río Tomeza Riverside Trail loop from Arcade&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 17.9 miles (28.9 km) through riverine and forested areas.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover along the hiking trails in Vilaboa?
A: Many hiking paths in Vilaboa incorporate significant historical attractions. You can encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is often a feature or starting point for longer hikes. Other notable landmarks in the wider region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galician Hórreo (Traditional Granary)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Pilgrim Virgin (Iglesia de la Virgen Peregrina)&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Pontevedra, a significant site along the Portuguese Way of St. James.

Q: Are there natural attractions like lakes or rivers to explore near Vilaboa's trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/488410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tomeza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight, guiding hikers along the Tomeza River through dense forests. You can also find lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1532292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Castiñeiras&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza da Moura&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene spots for relaxation.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Vilaboa?
A: The hiking routes in Vilaboa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the blend of riverine, coastal, and forested paths, and the integration of historical points of interest. The variety of trails, from easy strolls to more challenging treks, is also frequently appreciated.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vilaboa?
A: Vilaboa offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. However, it's worth noting that parts of the riverside paths, particularly along the Río Tomeza, can be prone to flooding and walkway damage during the rainy season. For the most comfortable experience, consider hiking during drier periods to fully enjoy all trail sections.

Q: Are there any trails that are part of the Portuguese Way of St. James in Vilaboa?
A: Yes, Vilaboa lies along variations of the Portuguese Way of St. James, a renowned long-distance pilgrimage route. Hikers can experience sections of this historic path, including stages that lead through areas like Tomeza and Ponte Sampaio, offering a glimpse into this significant cultural journey.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails in Vilaboa?
A: While specific named viewpoints aren't detailed, the diverse landscapes of Vilaboa naturally offer many scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14934538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats loop from Arcade&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the salt flats and Lake Castiñeiras, and the riverside trails along the Tomeza River offer picturesque forest and water vistas.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes in Vilaboa?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Vilaboa offers moderate to difficult trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Río Tomeza Riverside Trail loop from Arcade&lt;/a&gt;, a 17.9-mile (28.9 km) route with significant elevation changes, typically completed in about 7 hours 55 minutes. These routes require good fitness and sturdy footwear.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Vilaboa?
A: While specific trailhead parking details vary by route, areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5728483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo da Memoria Park&lt;/a&gt; are known to offer parking facilities, which can serve as convenient starting points for exploring the trails in the region.
tips: Very close to the Salinas de Ulló, it is necessary to leave the main track a few meters to visit the ruins.

Along the northern coast of the Ría de Vigo is an incredibly diverse natural landscape where the fresh water of the rivers meets the salt water of the San Simón coast, near Vilaboa. This swampy aquatic environment is attractive to explore.

faqs.question: Where is the starting point for the Las Salinas de Ulla — circular trail, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, meaning you can typically start at various points. A common access point would be near the Ulló Salt Flats, which are part of the Enseada de San Simón area. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, areas like this often have designated parking for visitors. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most precise parking locations.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike, covering approximately 6.3 kilometers, typically takes around 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. This duration allows for enjoying the scenery without feeling rushed.

faqs.question: Is the Las Salinas de Ulla — circular trail suitable for families with children or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is categorized as an easy hike, making it very suitable for families with children and beginners. Its relatively short distance and minimal elevation gain (around 87 meters) ensure a pleasant and accessible outdoor experience for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details for this exact trail are limited, similar 'Salinas' areas often feature well-demarcated footpaths designed to protect the natural environment. You can expect relatively flat and easy-to-navigate paths, likely through unique landscapes shaped by salt production and coastal ecosystems.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several interesting spots. Keep an eye out for the View of Lake Castiñeiras with wildflowers, the historical Ruins of the A Granxa farmhouse, and the unique Ulló Salt Flats themselves, which are a significant feature of the area.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter, especially in the salt flats area?
faqs.answer: The salt flats, like the Ulló Salt Flats, are unique ecosystems. While not explicitly stated for this trail, similar 'Salinas' environments are known for attracting various bird species, including flamingos, due to the specialized flora and crustaceans that thrive in high salt concentrations. Birdwatching can be a rewarding activity here.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and covers significant portions of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on sections of the R1: Ruta Enseada de San Simón Norte, Sendeiro de Croa, As Salinas, and PRG-104 Ruta da Croa, among others.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly, and are there any specific regulations?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas like the Enseada de San Simón park, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific regulations regarding dogs should be observed if posted at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Las Salinas de Ulla — circular?
faqs.answer: Given its coastal location and the nature of salt flats, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking this trail. The weather is typically milder, and you can avoid the peak heat of summer. These seasons also offer great opportunities for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail or the Enseada de San Simón park?
faqs.answer: Typically, access to natural areas and trails like the Las Salinas de Ulla — circular within the Enseada de San Simón park does not require permits or entrance fees. However, it's always a good practice to check for any local updates or specific regulations before your visit, especially for protected natural spaces.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural route, the Enseada de San Simón area and nearby towns like Vilaboa may offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restrooms. It's advisable to plan ahead and check for available facilities in the closest villages before starting your hike.

Ruins of the A Granxa farmhouse
